2003-06-01.title: Burmese Opposition Leader Taken Into Custody After Clashes
2003-06-01.abstract: Myanmar's military government takes Daw Aung San Suu Kyi and 19 of top members of her pro-democracy party into what it calls 'protective custody' after clash involving her supporters near town of Dipeyin that left 4 people dead and 50 injured; blames Aung San Suu Kyi for causing violence; also closes her party's headquarters in Yangon (M)

2003-06-01.title: German Chancellor's Economic Reform Plan Endorsed
2003-06-01.abstract: BERLIN, June 1  Chancellor Gerhard Schröder's plan to enact economic reform by reducing Germany's vast network of social protections was approved today by 90 percent of the delegates at a politically crucial meeting of his Social Democratic Party.

2003-06-01.title: With Protesters on the Move, Geneva Shops Board Up
2003-06-01.abstract: Most merchants have locked up and retreated in anticipation of possible violence surrounding the Group of 8 summit meeting that is to open in Évian on Sunday.

2003-06-01.title: U.S. Pushing Realignment of Troops in South Korea
2003-06-01.abstract: Deputy Defense Secretary Paul D. Wolfowitz indicated he hoped to convince South Korean that American troops should withdraw to south of Seoul.

2003-06-01.title: Over Protests, Nepal Lets Chinese Force Refugees Back to Tibet
2003-06-01.abstract: Nepal allows China to force 18 Tibetan refugees back to Tibet; has long winked at efforts by its ethnic neighbors in Tibet to separate from China, but is now bending more and more to pressure from Beijing, which is increasingly important aid and trade partner (M)

2003-06-02.title: BUSH PRESSES CASE ON IRAN AND KOREA AT ECONOMIC TALKS
2003-06-02.abstract: Pres Bush arrives in France for meeting of Group of 8, encountering European leaders who opposed war in Iraq and trying to increase pressure on Iran and North Korea to give up their nuclear weapons programs; offers pro forma handshakes to Pres Jacques Chirac of France and Chancellor Gerhard Schroder of Germany; Bush believes they undercut his effort against Saddam Hussein, but he shows no desire to rehash their differences; instead he sticks to main issue on his agenda--nonproliferation; his aides work behind scenes to sign countries up for new White House initiative to create international legal basis for seizing missiles, chemical and biological agents and nuclear components on high seas or in air; proposal is intended to give countries broader authority to quarantine North Korea, and perhaps Iran as well; it is unclear how many of traditionally American allies at meeting would agree; concerns about American empire can be heard within meeting halls and in huge protests moving through streets of Geneva and villages in both Switzerland and France; Bush will use economic forum to argue, as he already has, that world must unify behind American vision of confronting tyrants and unconventional weapons; photo; map (L)

2003-06-02.title: Spotlight Leaves Arafat, but He's Still in the Show
2003-06-02.abstract: Yasir Arafat's trappings of power are almost all gone, but Palestinian officials and analysts are certain he will be most influential Palestinian figure in renewed peace negotiations with Israel, even if he is working behind scenes; although Arafat does retain considerable influence, dynamics of Middle East conflict have been changing in month since Mahmoud Abbas assumed office as prime minister; Abbas's cabinet meets weekly without Arafat, and Abbas says he hopes to reach cease-fire deal with Islamic militant group Hamas and perhaps other radical Palestinian organizations, something Arafat was never able to deliver; Arafat is not invited to attend regional summit meeting in Jordan, when Pres Bush will meet with Abbas and Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon; but he remains head of Palestinian Authority, leader of Palestine Liberation Orgn and of Fatah movement; photo (M)

2003-06-02.title: Petitioners Urge China to Enforce Legal Rights
2003-06-02.abstract: Three young legal scholars in China send petition to national legislature, challenging system by which big-city police officers detain, fine and expel rural migrants at will, with no judicial oversight; their modest proposal is to enforce personal rights that are guaranteed in Constitution; petition has drawn unusual attention and praise in news media; it was submitted following unexplained beating death of man detained in Guangzhou, but drafters have larger goals in mind; they hope to advance cause of constitutional rights in general; in often paradoxical legal environment of China today, proposals that are tied to widely acknowledged social problems, that do not challenge one-party rule and that are couched in narrow terms of legal reform, can be debated more openly than ever before; photos (M)

2003-06-02.title: Arab Leaders Skeptical, but Hope for Results
2003-06-02.abstract: When President Bush opens a new Middle East peace quest on Tuesday in Egypt, he will confront a region still trying to absorb America's occupation of Iraq.

2003-06-02.title: Nepal's Maoist Guerrillas Let the Revolution Rest, for Now
2003-06-02.abstract: Nepal's Maoist guerrillas come to Katmandu under cease-fire that has virtually stopped political violence since it took effect four months ago; civil war that had festered out of sight of Katmandu exploded tenfold last year, claiming 4,655 lives and prompting King Gyanendra to fire his prime minister and make secret feelers to Maoists; Krishna Bahadur Mahara, Maoist leader, says there is now strategic equilibrium of military power; Maoists say they control 80 percent of countryside, while king oversees 50 percent increase in Royal Nepal Army, with new military aid arriving from India, Britain and United States; major question is whether Maoists are serious about talking peace or merely using time to gain military strength for new phase of war; photo (M)

2003-06-03.title: U.S.-French Détente: Leaders Lower Voices
2003-06-03.abstract: In their brief encounters in the French Alpine town of Évian-les-Bains over the past two days, George W. Bush and Jacques Chirac acted like grown-ups.    There were no testy exchanges, no biting one-liners, no disparaging comments by unidentified aides. On the contrary, when the American and French presidents met for the first time since their bitter split over the American-led war on Iraq, they were polite in public and, by all accounts, even nice to each other in private.

2003-06-03.title: Pakistani Legislators Approve Islamic Law for Province
2003-06-03.abstract: The provincial assembly here, dominated by an alliance of religious parties, voted unanimously today to introduce Islamic law to the North-West Frontier Province, fulfilling an election promise that has worried the national government and its American allies.    The six-party religious alliance, the Muttahida Majlis-e-Amal, which holds a majority in the assembly, is opposed to the American presence in Afghanistan and is sympathetic to Taliban elements that are continuing to fight United States forces. It has campaigned to introduce Islamic law, or Shariah, to ensure better justice for the population, but critics accuse it of dragging the province back into the Taliban era.

2003-06-03.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-06-03.abstract: INDIA: Dangerous Heat; PHILIPPINES: Pact With U.S. On Tribunals; SINGAPORE: Conjoined Adults To Be Separated;.

2003-06-03.title: Senate Panel Inquiry on Iraq Weapons
2003-06-03.abstract: With questions persisting about the failure to find unconventional weapons in Iraq, Senator John W. Warner, Republican of Virginia and chairman of the Armed Services Committee, said today that his panel would conduct hearings this month on the issue.    The House Intelligence Committee has already requested that the C.I.A. investigate whether prewar assessments of Iraq's weapons capacity were flawed.

2003-06-03.title: Bush, in Egypt, Finds Warmth And Wariness
2003-06-03.abstract: President Bush arrived in the Mideast tonight for the first time in his presidency, preparing to face Arab leaders skeptical of American intentions in Iraq and to mediate between the Israelis and the Palestinians, a role he has long avoided.    When Air Force One landed here tonight on the southern tip of the Sinai Peninsula, Mr. Bush was greeted with a hug by the Egyptian president, Hosni Mubarak. But the warm welcome masked undercurrents of wariness.

2003-06-04.title: Hoping a Troubled Bridge Over Water Will Be a Pathway to Mideast Peace
2003-06-04.abstract: Israeli and Palestinian prime ministers will appear together publicly for first time in Aqaba, Jordan, in company of Pres Bush; White House operatives get Jordanians to build bridge over pool at king's new palace to enable leaders to walk together toward massed cameras for literal image and metaphor; photo; Mahmoud Abbas is expected to make explicit call for end of armed intifada; Ariel Sharon to endorse vision of two states living in peace (M)

2003-06-04.title: Ex-head of army warns of long stay and says Pentagon balks at admitting it : U.S. leader in Iraq sees role for the UN
2003-06-04.abstract: The head of the U.S.-led administration in Iraq met Tuesday with the new United Nations representative to Iraq, and they vowed to work together "to create a democratic, independent and peaceful Iraq."

2003-06-04.title: Beside Blossoming Fields, Where Poverty Grows
2003-06-04.abstract: Shores of broad lake that spreads across Africa's Rift Valley in Kenya, where British colonials once disported on emerald lawns and verandas, are now setting for booming industry in growing out-of-season vegetables and cut flowers that are shipped to distant markets in Europe; photo; enormous tracts of farmland have drawn thousands of workers, mostly women from distant villages who earn barely enough to survive; proponents of globalization say self-interest of business will lead to better wages and environment for workers (M)

2003-06-04.title: World Briefing | Africa: Egypt: Journalists Jailed For Libel
2003-06-04.abstract: Egyptian court upholds one-year sentence for libel against brothers Mustafa Bakri, editor in chief of weekly newspaper Al Osboa, and Mahmoud Bakri who works at paper; libel charges stem from articles published 3 years ago about personal life of opposition Social Justice Party leader Muhammad Abdel-Aal, who is currently serving 10-year sentence for accepting bribes (S)

2003-06-04.title: Blair Denies Britain Distorted Intelligence Reports on Iraq
2003-06-04.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Blair said today a parliamentary committee would investigate the accusations and make public its findings.

2003-06-05.title: In Border Town, Migrant Crackdown Rankles
2003-06-05.abstract: Authorities conduct raids in Altar, Mexico, shutting down dozens of rundown hostels that serve illegal migrants waiting to cross border into United States; raids are latest step in crackdown by Pres Vicente Fox against criminal groups, characterized as international mafias, that have made immigrant smuggling billion dollars a year industry; law enforcement officials estimate that in this region of border alone, 1,500 people attempt to cross desert each day, and scores die each year, most from exposure; map; photo (M)

2003-06-05.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-06-05.abstract: VIETNAM: Crime Boss Convicted; NEPAL: New Prime Minister Appointed; TAIWAN: Crash Analysis Issued;.

2003-06-05.title: Looking Beyond Words
2003-06-05.abstract: News analsysis: commitments secured by Pres Bush from Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon and Palestinian Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas to road map for peace will require both sides to face up to their internal conflicts; Sharon's promise to begin confronting settler movement he nurtured for decades means plunging nation into long-postponed national debate over what Israel wants from its occupation of West Bank and Gaza; Abbas's promise to end armed Palestinian uprising means stopping terrorists who have highacked Palestinian national dream and shifting internal Palestinian conversation away from empathy for suicide bombers; both leaders will face severe challenges, which are already beginning, as thousands of settlers stage angry protest and Hamas leaders reject Abbas's suggestion that they surrender their arms; photos (M)

2003-06-05.title: European Peacekeepers to Go To Congo on Non-NATO Mission
2003-06-05.abstract: European Union governments agree to send joint peacekeeping force to Congo to help contain ethnic violence; 1,400-member force, which was authorized by United Nations, marks first time European Union will carry out military operation without help from North Atlantic Treaty Organization and first European Union military operation outside of Europe (M)

2003-06-05.title: Another G.I. Dies in Violence-Prone Iraqi Town
2003-06-05.abstract: The attack in Falluja, which also injured five U.S. troops, was the eighth death of an American soldier in the last 10 days.

2003-06-06.title: G.I.'s Will Gradually Leave Korea DMZ to Cut War Risk
2003-06-06.abstract: United States and South Korea agree to reposition American troops far away from demilitarized zone that separates North and South Korea to make them less vulnerable to attack by North; 14,000 American troops are considered strategic 'tripwire' on Korean peninsula, ensuring that American troops would be drawn into any war started by North; redeployment, which will occur over period of years, is negotiated at time of high tensions beetween US and North Korea over North's suspected development of nuclear weapons; it comes after months of strained relations between US and South Korea, where Pres Roh Moo Hyun won election in December amid large-scale protest movement against American military presence in country; most of 37,000 American troops in South Korea are based in northern tier of country, in and around Seoul; some South Korean officials feared that repositioning of troops would leave US free to make pre-emptive attack against North Korea's nuclear sites without fear of retaliation against American soldiers; their fears are eased when US pledges that some American troops will remain presence in northern part of country, near demilitarized zone; photo (M)

2003-06-06.title: Leading Iraqi Shiite Cleric Emerges to Meet U.S. Ally
2003-06-06.abstract: Grand Ayatollah Ali al-Sestani, one of most senior Shiite clerics in Iraq and world, steps into political fray; meets with Kurdish leader Massoud Barzani, who has enjoyed close ties to Washington, and calls for elections to national assembly for Iraqis to produce new constitution; Sestani is reportedly critical of postwar conditions in Iraq; his visit with Barzani underscores fact that dissatisfaction with current state of planning in Washington and London for postwar government extends beyond former Iraqi opposition leaders to other major figures (M)

2003-06-06.title: Another Attack in Central Iraq Kills Another U.S. Soldier
2003-06-06.abstract: Hidden assailants in Falluja, Iraq, launch another attack on American soldiers, killing one American and wounding five; after series of almost weekly attacks on American forces, Falluja is emerging as center of anti-American resistance in Iraq; US commanders adopt get-tough approach in city; 4,000 soldiers will replace 1,200-member squadron; what occurs in Falluja in next several weeks could set precedent; American military might could either crush dissent in area--or fuel it; photo of one resident who says he was roughly searched by soldiers; map (M)

2003-06-06.title: Vatican Traveler in Croatia, Reaching 100, Trips, That Is
2003-06-06.abstract: Pope John Paul II's visit to Croatia marks 100th time he has left Vatican City for foreign visit, reminder of extent to which travel has defined his papacy and he has used travel to redefine papacy itself; since beginning of his papacy in 1978, he has traveled to more than 125 countries, many of them more than once; his frequent trips, coupled with news coverage it generates, has given him enhanced stature on international stage; Vatican officials say he considers travel integral part of his mission; photo of Pope with Croation Pres Stjepan Mesic (M)

2003-06-06.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-06-06.abstract: GEORGIA: U.N. Observers Kidnapped; EUROPEAN UNION PENALIZES CUBA;.

2003-06-07.title: U.N. Envoy Will Try to Visit Burmese Dissident Held by Junta
2003-06-07.abstract: A United Nations envoy arrived today in Myanmar and said he would press for the release of the pro-democracy leader Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, who is in detention after a violent clash that the United States said might have been instigated by the government.    Arriving on a previously scheduled visit, the envoy, Razali Ismail, said he hoped to be allowed to see Mrs. Aung San Suu Kyi as well as top government officials. She has been held at a secret location since the incident on Friday in which the government said 4 people were killed and 50 injured.

2003-06-07.title: As Iraqis' Disaffection Grows, U.S. Offers Them a Greater Political Role
2003-06-07.abstract: The imam at one of Baghdad's largest mosques urged more than a thousand listeners today to wage a jihad, or religious war, against American occupying forces in Iraq.    Speaking at Friday Prayers, Imam Mouaid al-Ubaidi denounced the Americans as invaders and aggressors and implicitly praised recent guerrilla attacks against United States soldiers as self-defense by people who are being ''strangled.''

2003-06-07.title: HAMAS BREAKS OFF TALKS ON STOPPING ATTACKS ON ISRAEL
2003-06-07.abstract: Ismail Abu Shanab and other leaders of militant Islamic group Hamas break off talks with Palestinian Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas on cease-fire with Israel; cite his speech at meeting with Israel demanding end to armed uprising and expressing compassion for Jewish suffering, interviews; Abbas supporters say Hamas seeks to exploit unpopular speech, drafted by Bush administration, to oust help oust him; photo (M)

2003-06-07.title: Some Analysts Of Iraq Trailers Reject Germ Use
2003-06-07.abstract: American and British intelligence analysts with direct access to evidence dispute claims that trailers found in Iraq in Apr and May were part of biological warfare program, interviews; say they were more likely intended for other purposes; charge evaluation process was damaged by rush to judgment; Central Intelligence Agency reaffirms stand (M)

2003-06-07.title: New increases expected as vacations begins : Still paying a high price for a 'roaming' cell phone
2003-06-07.abstract: PARIS:—  Ted Matheny, a lawyer based in Paris, travels about one week a month for business, racking up mobile phone charges of about €400 a month.

2003-06-08.title: U.S. Troops Put On a Show of Military Force in a Center of Hard-Core Resistance
2003-06-08.abstract: US tanks rumble through Falluja, Iraq, in display for force intended to reassert control of unruly city; soldiers are trying to root out enemy fighters who have killed three Americans and wounded more than dozen before fading back into narrow streets; photo (M)

2003-06-08.title: Forgive, Pope Says, But Croats Find It Hard
2003-06-08.abstract: Croatians of Vukovar, where Serbs killed 5,000 people in siege nearly twelve years ago, find it difficult to heed Pope John Paul II's call for forgiveness; photo of pope in Osijek; map (M)

2003-06-08.title: Shiite Muslim Party Warns It May Boycott Iraq Council
2003-06-08.abstract: Leading Shiite Muslim party, Islamic Revolution in Iraq, says it will not join governing council if members are appointed by US administrator Paul Bremer; US soldier is killed and four others wounded when gunmen open fire near Tikrit; Gen Muhammad Habib al-Mashadani, former deputy commander of national police, is arrested on charges of trying to re-create Baath Party cell within reconstituted police force (M)

2003-06-08.title: Ethnic Militias in Congo Battle for Control of a Town
2003-06-08.abstract: Lendu and Hema gunmen battle for control of Congo town of Bunia; photo (S)

2003-06-08.title: Indians Pursue Portuguese Passports as an Entree to Europe
2003-06-08.abstract: Young Indians in Goa, colonized by Portugal five centuries ago, are taking advantage of Portuguese law granting them citizenship to obtain passports that also get them into European Union countries; Portugal gave up colony in 1961 (M)

2003-06-09.title: Afghan Youth's Death Raises Kabul Bombing Toll to Five
2003-06-09.abstract: Afghan teenager wounded in suicide car bomb attack on German peacekeepers dies, bringing to five those killed in attack; is lone Afghan casualty, apart from bomber; four German soldiers are killed and 31 wounded; it is worst attack on international peacekeeping force in 18 months it has been deployed in Kabul and surrounding area; photo (M)

2003-06-09.title: British Finance Official Says Euro Vote Is Possible
2003-06-09.abstract: The chancellor of the exchequer said today that Britons might vote in a referendum next year on joining the euro

2003-06-09.title: Abbas Answers Critics as Israel Shuts Down a Few Outposts
2003-06-09.abstract: The Palestinian prime minister said today that there was no alternative to the new peace plan while Israel began taking down a handful of mostly uninhabited settlements.

2003-06-09.title: For Pope and the Croats, Superstar's Muted Echo
2003-06-09.abstract: Pope John Paul II marks his 100th foreign trip with visit to Croatia; visit's muted volume and modest scale, at least by standard of many previous papal journeys, underscore degree to which his infirmities and longevity have begun to lend different shade to his adventures; crowds in Croatian port city of Rijeka respond to him more respectfully than raucously, with softer chants and cheers; overarching atmosphere is more subdued, in line with his diminished energies and less expansive gestures; photo (M)

2003-06-09.title: Sharon and Abbas, Both Under Fire From Hard-Liners
2003-06-09.abstract: Members of Prime Min Ariel Sharon's Likud Party, attending party convention in Jerusalem, boo and heckle in latest round of criticism he is enduring from traditional right-wing allies in wake of his proposed concessions to Palestinians; they accuse him of surrendering to terror; in Ramallah, Palestinian Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas faces his own barrage of criticism from hard-liners and moderates alike who accuse him of being too conciliatory toward Israel; Sharon is politically strong and Abbas is vulnerable in his new post, but both face mounting internal pressure that threatens latest Middle East peace plan before first steps have been taken to put it into effect; photo (M)

2003-06-10.title: Hunting Clues, Anthrax Investigators Drain Maryland Pond
2003-06-10.abstract: Federal authorities begin draining small pond in municipal park near Frederick, Md, searching for evidence in unsolved anthrax mailings that killed five people and inflicted illness on 17 others in 2001; plastic box that some bioterrorism experts say could have been used to carry anthrax was retrieved from pond last Dec; pond is eight miles from Army Medical Research Institute of Infectious Diseases at Fort Detrick, Md; inquiry has become one of largest ever by FBI and postal authorities; they have unearthed thousands of leads, but they have publicly singled out only Dr Steven Hatfill as 'person of interest'; he is germ warfare expert who once worked at Fort Detrick lab and lived in Frederick; has repeatedly said he was not involved in any way with anthrax letters; photo; map (M)

2003-06-10.title: Bush Again Vows U.S. Will Find Illicit Weapons
2003-06-10.abstract: Pres Bush says he is 'absolutely convinced' that United States will find proof that Iraq has chemical and biological weapons programs, but he stops short of declaring that Iraq had weapons ready for use rather than just programs to develop them; brushes aside any doubts about primary justification he offered before war for removing Saddam Hussein from power; defends decision to go to war, and again says there are ties between Al Qaeda and Hussein's government; lack of firm evidence that Iraq possessed banned weapons in form that could be used against allied troops or passed to terrorists is becoming foreign policy and political challenge for White House (M)

2003-06-10.title: British Envoy Sees Allies Running Iraq In Near Term
2003-06-10.abstract: John Sawers, Britain's representative to American administration in Baghdad, says occupying powers intend to run country for foreseeable future, with Iraqis restricted to advisory role; plan he outlines draws sharp criticism from Iraqi political groups that supported American-led invasion and had expected to help govern country, or at least appoint their own consultative council, once Saddam Hussein was deposed; Sawers notes that Iraqis who are beginning to chafe at American and British occupation would also object to transitional government chosen exclusively by former exile groups; says Iraqis who do take part in interim administration must be generally representative of Iraq as a whole; photo (M)

2003-06-10.title: Stun Guns Closer for Planes
2003-06-10.abstract: Transportation Security Adm says in report to Congress that stun guns may be permitted on airplanes to protect against hijackers; United Airlines and Mesa Air Group has asked agency to consider allowing their pilots to carry such weapons; agency has not issued final decision (S)

2003-06-10.title: World Briefing | Europe: Georgia: Talks With Kidnappers
2003-06-10.abstract: Georgian officials talk with kidnappers of four United Nations military observers monitoring Georgia and Abkhazia cease-fire, but are unable to recover two Germans, Dane and Georgian translator held by captors demanding $3 million in ransom (S)

2003-06-11.title: U.S. Troops in Afghanistan Kill 4 Taliban Suspects Near Border
2003-06-11.abstract: American troops in Afghanistan kill four suspected Taliban members in gun battle on Pakistan border; no US casualties reported (S)

2003-06-11.title: Bush Asks Nations to Renounce Mideast Violence
2003-06-11.abstract: President Bush condemned the latest deadly bus bombing in Israel today and urged "all the free world" to renounce violence between Israelis and Palestinians.

2003-06-11.title: Japan Detains 2 North Korean Ships, Part of Pressure Strategy
2003-06-11.abstract: Japan detains two North Korean cargo ships in Japanese ports for safety inspections, day after US encourages allies to pressure North Korea into negotiating end to its nuclear bomb program (S)

2003-06-11.title: Singapore Tightens SARS-Control System
2003-06-11.abstract: Singapore toughens its already strict SARS-prevention regimen (S)

2003-06-11.title: From farm to fork, risks persist
2003-06-11.abstract: BRUSSELS:—  The dangers lurk in the unlikeliest places.

2003-06-12.title: Venezuelan Opposition Courts Working Class
2003-06-12.abstract: Large but unwieldy opposition out to end Pres Hugo Chavez's tumultuous rule in Venezuela is adopting new strategy; it is courting country's working class, people in poor neighborhoods who distrust opposition and view it as part of privileged class; Chavez has helped create sharp divide between many of poor and opposition; this has made it difficult for government foes to freely express themselves in hillside slums where he still has strong support, because of threat of violence; opposition has found that rallies backfire in poor areas; their new tact is to go door to door, taking time to explain their position; referendum on Chavez's presidency could take place later this year; photo (M)

2003-06-12.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Killings Of Defense Executives Linked
2003-06-12.abstract: Russian businessman Igor Klimov, recently appointed by government to consolidate air-defense companies, and Sergei Shchitko, official at air-defense company involved in merger, are both shot within few hours of each other in different parts of Moscow; suspected assassinations are part of current wave of killings targeting politicians and businessmen (S)

2003-06-12.title: Vandalism and 'Improvements' Mar Great Wall
2003-06-12.abstract: Chinese experts survey 101 sites along Great Wall, China's most cherished landmark, and voice alarm at what they find; in western regions, where Wall was largely constructed of mud, erosion has weathered it and sand has buried it in many areas; in eastern portion of Wall, human activities have caused most damage: graffiti on Wall at site near Beijing, and portions of Wall bulldozed in Inner Mongolia to make way for construction; in many locations, Wall has been gussied up with Disney-like creations, in misguided local attempts at 'renovating' ancient Wall to bring tourism into area; Great Wall traverses nine provinces and 100 counties; photo; map (M)

2003-06-12.title: Bush Under Fire in Congress for Criticizing Israel
2003-06-12.abstract: On a day of new attacks and counterattacks, there was concern in the Bush administration that the peace plan known as the road map could founder.

2003-06-12.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Egypt: 'Matrix' Film Banned
2003-06-12.abstract: Egyptian Censorship Director Madkur Thabet bans Matrix Reloaded from Egyptian cinemas because he contends film's violence threatens social peace and content presents controversial view of human creation (S)

2003-06-13.title: Kurds and Displaced Arabs Compete for Land
2003-06-13.abstract: Hundreds of Arab families in Iraq's north are being displaced by Kurds, who themselves were driven from area during Iraq's most sweeping ethnic cleansing campaign over 30 years ago; since war, Kurds are returning to their original homes and farmland, and Arabs are migrating back to lands in center, south and west of Iraq; some Arab families have no home or land to go back to; land disputes in north were foreseen by relief organizations long before war (M)

2003-06-13.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: A Soldier's Life For Harry
2003-06-13.abstract: Prince Harry, youngest son of Prince Charles and Diana, late Princess of Wales, completes course of study at Eton and royal family announces his application to Sandhurst Royal Military Academy to begin army career; like his older brother William, he plans to spend year abroad before school; photo (S)

2003-06-13.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-06-13.abstract: BRITAIN: A Soldier'S Life For Harry; BRITAIN: Blair Shuffles Ministers; BRITAIN: Firefighters Settle Dispute; FRANCE: Strikes Fizzle; CZECH REPUBLIC: Bribery On Jet Deal Alleged;.

2003-06-13.title: Envoys Hopefully Hit Road On Behalf of Imperiled Map
2003-06-13.abstract: Bush administration mobilizes to keep its Middle East peace initiative from unraveling; Secretary of State Colin L Powell and other envoys will travel to region to seek mutual concessions from Israel and Palestinians; Powell speaks on telephone to Prime Min Ariel Sharon and Palestinian Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas, urging that efforts be made to get peace talks back on track; John S Wolf, new American envoy to Middle East, is scheduled to leave for Israel on June 13 as part of team to monitor progress on implementing peace plan; Bush administration is frustrated that Arab leaders have not followed through with their pledge to cut off support for Palestinian militant groups responsible for acts of violence against Israelis; photo (M)

2003-06-13.title: Israeli Assault on Hamas Leaves 7 More Dead
2003-06-13.abstract: Israel's Army escalates battle with Hamas militants, blasting Palestinian car with helicopter-fired missiles in Gaza City and killing seven people, including Yasir Taha, Hamas leader, his wife and child; attack comes as Israelis and Palestinians are still burying their dead from carnage day before; in grimly familiar pattern, signs of diplomatic progress have been greeted almost immediately with surge in violence throughout 32 months of fighting; government-owned Israeli Radio says army has been ordered to 'completely wipe out' Hamas movement after suicide bombing of bus in Jerusalem; 17th victim of that bombing dies of wounds; Israel says new Palestinian Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas has proved unable or unwilling to rein in militants since he assumed office six weeks ago; Palestinians say Israeli actions are badly undercutting Abbas, making it impossible for him to resume cease-fire talks with Palestinian militants; he has ruled out using force against them; Hamas says violence is likely to worsen, and it calls on foreigners to leave Israel; White House blames Palestinian extremist for violence; photos; map (L)

2003-06-14.title: U.S. Faces a Long Fight With Hussein Partisans
2003-06-14.abstract: News analysis of long, hard resistance Americans face from Saddam Hussein loyalists; says that while Hussein may not be directing insurgency, nor even be alive, he provides rallying point for former Baathists, many of whom are plotting return to power as part of movement called Return Party (M)

2003-06-14.title: Iraqi Pipeline Blast Is Laid To Sabotage
2003-06-14.abstract: Adel Qazzaz, director general of Iraq's Northern Oil Co, says explosion along major oil pipeline route in north-central Iraq appears to have been act of sabotage; incident comes as State Oil Marketing Organization, which oversees export of Iraqi oil, announces companies that will buy Iraq's first oil exports since war (M)

2003-06-14.title: CONTEMPORARY ART:A SPECIAL REPORT : A young artist in Umbria re-examines contours of Photorealism
2003-06-14.abstract: TODI, Italy—  Of all recent art movements, few were execrated by the modernists so virulently as Photorealism. The painters and sculptors of this school, which emerged in the United States in the mid-1960s, produced more or less exact copies of photographs (or in the case of sculptures, often direct body casts), recording seemingly banal city scenes, suburban streets, storefronts, diners, consumer goods, automobiles and motorcycles, with phenomenal adeptness, but with a disconcerting level of emotional detachment.

2003-06-14.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Court Backs Veteran On Gulf Illness
2003-06-14.abstract: Czech Republic Television survey shows voters overwhelmingly support membership in European Union with 84% of those asked casting yes ballots for referendum (S)

2003-06-14.title: Police in Thailand Seize Radioactive Material
2003-06-14.abstract: Authorities in Thailand arrest Narong Penanam after seizing large amount of radioactive material he was selling for use in so-called dirty bombs, breaking up plot to sell it to terrorists; material is cesium-137, radioactive byproduct of nuclear power plants commonly found in medical equipment; law enforcement and terrorism experts express alarm that material is available on black market; Penanam, who has no known ties to terrorist groups, is caught in sting operation (M)

2003-06-15.title: Blair Seeks a Supreme Court Modeled on the U.S. Version
2003-06-15.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair's government proposes creation of American-style Supreme Court and independent commission to appoint judges in move to free up Britain's judicial system from political control (M)

2003-06-15.title: U.S. FORCES LAUNCH RAIDS IN IRAQ CITIES TO QUELL UPRISINGS
2003-06-15.abstract: Thousands of American troops backed by tanks, planes and helicopters carry out extensive raids in restive city of Falluja and in at least two other Iraqi cities; military operation is one of largest in Iraq since end of major fighting; no American casualties are reported; dozens of suspected Baath Party members are detained; map; photo (M)

2003-06-15.title: Pentagon Officials Say They See No Organized Iraqi Resistance
2003-06-15.abstract: Pentagon officials monitoring attacks on American forces in Iraq say that there are no indications of a centrally organized resistance movement.

2003-06-15.title: Marines Steam to Liberia
2003-06-15.abstract: More than 2,000 United States marines aboard amphibious assault ship Kearsarge steam toward west coast of Africa to prepare for possible evacuation of American citizens from strife-torn Liberia; more than 500 foreigners are evacuated in week from Monrovia (S)

2003-06-15.title: EUROPE DRAFTING ITS CONSTITUTION
2003-06-15.abstract: European Union delegates struggle to draft its first constitution; 105 delegates who make up Convention on Future of Europe try to codify common ground among long-established states that will give their union more of a logical structure--and perhaps more power--as they expand eastward; draft constitution states that every national of a member state shall be citizen of union; when union expands, that would mean mega-Europe of 450 million citizens, and economy of more than $9 trillion; proposed constitution states that European Union law will have primacy over that of member states; simplifies voting rules and spells out areas like trade policy in which union will have full authority and other areas to be shared with member states, including justice, transportation and economic and social policy; photo of president of European Convention, Valery Giscard de'Estaing of France, and his vice presidents, Jean Luc Dehaene of Belgium and Giuliani Amato of Italy; map (L)

2003-06-16.title: Sharon Vows Tough Line After Hamas Rejects Cease-Fire
2003-06-16.abstract: The Israeli leader's tough talk came only hours after an Egyptian delegation failed to win a cease-fire pledge from Palestinian militants.

2003-06-16.title: European Union Toughening Stance on Weapons Policy
2003-06-16.abstract: European Union governments said today that they were ready to use force if necessary to combat the threat of weapons of mass destruction.

2003-06-16.title: Young Iranians Are Chafing Under Aging Clerics' Edicts
2003-06-16.abstract: Official Iran is pitted against the kind of society in which most Iranians want to live.

2003-06-16.title: Behind Brazil's Leftist Leader, a Kindred Spirit Thrives
2003-06-16.abstract: Jose Dirceu de Oliveira e Sliva, chief of staff of Pres Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva of Brazil, is generally regarded as second most powerful man in Brazil, acting as political coordinator and gatekeeper of government; power is enhanced by fact that da Silva's keeps his distance from minutiae of government; Dirceu says government's commitment to improve, develop and radicalize Brazilian democracy has not changed, even though it is maintaining policies his party criticized when it was in opposition; remains suspicious of US, but American officials say Dirceu has been coopertaive and pragmatic in dealing with them; photo (M)

2003-06-16.title: Hurt in Body and Mind, A Lawyer Battles On
2003-06-16.abstract: Lahib Naoman, 48-year-old Iraqi lawyer, has paid the price of running afoul of Saddam Hussein, beginning 20 years ago when she defended man accused of stealing videocassette recorder belonging to Hussein's son Uday; has spent 17 years in and out of Baghdad's prison and psychiatric hospitals; now lives in penury in a near empty house; photo (M)

2003-06-17.title: Hunt Imperils Polar Bears In Bering Sea, Report Says
2003-06-17.abstract: Federal wildlife biologists in United States say polar bears that traverse floating ice between Russian Arctic and Alaska are being shot in rising numbers by poachers on Russian shores; say killing could greatly diminish bear population if it is not slowed; findings are released as US and Russia consider ratifying treaty they signed in 2000 to protect shared population of about 4,000 bears; treaty would allow limited hunting by native populations but would clamp down on poaching under management plan developed by scientists and representatives of native communities; Pres Bush has urged Senate to approve treaty (M)

2003-06-17.title: Sharon Vows to 'Hound' Hamas, Which Rejects a Cease-Fire Bid
2003-06-17.abstract: Prime Min Ariel Sharon, addressing Israel's Parliament, warns that Middle East diplomacy will stall if 'terror runs rampant,' and pledges to 'hound the terrorists' until Palestinian security forces crack down on them; speech comes only hours after visiting Egyptian delegation fails to win cease-fire pledge from Hamas and other militant Palestinian factions during two days of talks in Gaza Strip; Palestinian Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas arrives in Gaza City for new talks with Hamas, which has rebuffed his previous appeals for truce; Palestinian officials say they are caught between widely divergent demands of Israel and Hamas; each side wants other to agree to truce first; nevertheless, Israeli and Palestinian officials renew security talks; further talks are considered likely on proposal for withdrawing Israeli troops from Palestinian areas in northern Gaza, with Palestinian security forces replacing them; violence remains constant threat, with Hamas refusing to suspend its bombing campaign and with Israeli troops still operating in and around Palestinian cities; Sharon' s speech focuses on security and is directed at his core of right-wing supporters who are deeply skeptical of peacemaking efforts after 32 months of almost daily fighting; he is being criticized by both left and right; photo (M)

2003-06-17.title: Iran Is Urged To Sign Pact Giving Power To Inspectors
2003-06-17.abstract: Iran is urged to sign protocol that would give United Nations nuclear weapons inspectors right to conduct more intrusive examinations of its facilities; both Iran's main trading partner, European Union, and Russia, whose engineers are helping it build civilian nuclear power plant, call on Tehran to provide necessary cooperation; calls come after governing board of International Atomic Energy Agency formally reviews recent report that gives new details about Iranian plans for heavy water research reactor, which could be used in production of plutonium for nuclear weapons; US State Dept responds to developments by calling on board of IAEA to formally urge Iran to sign protocol, which was added to Nuclear Nonproliferation Treaty (M)

2003-06-17.title: In Speech, Bush Reiterates Threat Hussein Posed, but Makes No Mention of Weapons Search
2003-06-17.abstract: Pres Bush sweeps aside criticism that United States overstated threat posed by Iraq as work of 'revisionist historians' and says there is no doubt that Saddam Hussein posed 'threat to the United States' for past dozen years; speaks to small-business owners in Elizabeth, NJ, dwelling largely on benefits of recently passed tax cuts; makes no mention of so-far fruitless search for Hussein's chemical, nuclear or biological weapons; his speech is seen as harbinger of his approach to early days of his re-election campaign; his decision not to delve into question of unconventional weapons comes 11 days after he addressed American troops in Qatar, insisting then that his concerns about Hussein's weapons were justified; photo (M)

2003-06-17.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: Warrant For Marine In Rape Case
2003-06-17.abstract: Japanese police obtain arrest warrant for Lance Cpl Jose W Torres, United States Marine accused of raping 19-year-old woman on island of Okinawa on May 25; he is now in military custody (S)

2003-06-18.title: Palestinians Raise Hope of Cease-Fire Deal With Militants
2003-06-18.abstract: Palestinian officials say they are nearing cease-fire agreement with three groups, including Hamas, that are responsible for most terrorist attacks against Israel; 7-year-old Israeli girl is killed and 5-year-old sister seriously wounded when Palestinian gunman opens fire on car inside Israel, northeast of Tel Aviv; photo; Mahmoud Zahar of Hamas says it is 'proper time for hudna,' using Arabic word for truce, but not without long list of concessions, including release of prisoners, withdrawal of Israeli forces from much of West Bank and Gaza and end of assassinations of militants (M)

2003-06-18.title: Rights Group Says U.S. Soldiers Twice Used Excessive Force
2003-06-18.abstract: Human Rights Watch accuses US troops of using excessive force in two April shootings that killed 20 Iraqis and wounded at least 86; soldiers were responding to hostile fire from protesters in Falluja (S)

2003-06-18.title: As SARS Ebbs, W.H.O. Lifts Its Advisory On Taiwan
2003-06-18.abstract: WHO lifts advisory against travel to Taiwan, leaving Beijing as only place travelers are urged to avoid because of SARS risk; Taiwan has had five or fewer new cases every day this month (S)

2003-06-18.title: Plan to Block North Korean Nuclear Shipments Gains Support
2003-06-18.abstract: Sec Colin L Powell says US has broad support to press North Korea to abandon nuclear weapons program; comments in Cambodia, where Assn of South East Asian Nations and Asian Regional Forum will discuss efforts against regime; US move to interdict shipments of nuclear materials draws new threat of retaliation from North Korea (M)

2003-06-18.title: Now the Dirtiest of Wars Won't Be Forgotten
2003-06-18.abstract: Letter From South America reports that Pres Nestor Kirchner is carrying out inaugural pledge to seek justice for estimated 30,000 people who disappeared during 'dirty war' of military dictatorship between 1976 and 1983; photo of mass grave; Kirchner purges military command, says he will allow extradition of human rights violators wanted in other countries and makes clear he wants Supreme Court to overturn amnesty laws and pardons (M)

2003-06-19.title: Beckham's eye-catching knack for the soft sell
2003-06-19.abstract: LONDON:—  The David and Victoria Beckham phenomenon, having left Hollywood somewhat underwhelmed, touched down at Narita Airport in Tokyo on Wednesday to the tumult of a thousand shrieking girls, fans of the soccer player.

2003-06-19.title: Parliament In Italy Passes Immunity Law For Berlusconi
2003-06-19.abstract: Italian Parliament grants Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi immunity from prosecution while he remains in office, effectively derailing his corruption trial just days before he assumes rotating presidency of European Union; Pres Carlo Azeglio Ciami is expected sign law quickly (M)

2003-06-19.title: Powell to Visit Israel to Ask for Looser Reins in Gaza
2003-06-19.abstract: The secretary of state said that he would press for a transfer of security operations in the northern Gaza Strip to Palestinians.

2003-06-19.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Writer-Politician To Be Freed
2003-06-19.abstract: Russian court orders parole of Eduard Limonov, writer who came to lead ultranationalist National Bolshevik Party and who was sentenced in April to four years in prison for weapons possession (S)

2003-06-19.title: 'Lost Boy' Shines Light on Race in South Africa
2003-06-19.abstract: Happy Sindane becomes national sensation after South African teenager walks into Bronkhorstspruit police station and tells officer that he is white and was kidnapped from his Afrikaner parent as young child and virtually enslaved in black township; police are flooded with callers claiming to be his parents, and court orders DNA tests in hopes of finding resolution; case raises legacy of apartheid and country's obsession with racial makeup, not only as symbol of white fears of being taken over by black majority but also because one focus of DNA testing is to determine whether Happy is of mixed race; map; photos (M)

2003-06-20.title: Draft of European Union's Constitution Wins General Support
2003-06-20.abstract: European leaders broadly endorsed the draft of a first-ever constitution for the European Union at a summit meeting here today.

2003-06-20.title: Hussein Is Probably Alive in Iraq, U.S. Experts Say, Citing Intercepts
2003-06-20.abstract: American intelligence analysts now believe that Saddam Hussein is more likely alive than dead, view reinforced in recent weeks by intercepted communications among some of his fugitive supporters; new hunt for Hussein is being led by Task Force 20, secret military organization that includes members of Army's highly specialized Delta Force and Navy's elite counterterrorism squads, with support from Central Intelligence Agency; prevailing belief is that Hussein is still in Iraq; arrest of Abid Hamid Mahmoud al-Tikriti, Hussein's closest confidant, has raised some hope of obtaining more conclusive information; but Mahmoud's success in eluding capture for nearly two months in country occupied by nearly 150,000 troops underscores reality that Iraq still offers many hiding places; chronology of developments since March 20 in trying to determine Hussein's fate; photos (M)

2003-06-20.title: Discarded War Munitions Leach Poisons Into the Baltic
2003-06-20.abstract: Thousands of war munitions dumped by American, British and Soviet military after World War II are leaching toxic chemicals into Baltic Sea as weapon casings corrode; even entire ships full of weapons, most captured from Germany, were scuttled and forgotten; problem is compounded by fisherman who go to risky areas to catch depleted fish stocks, using increasingly aggressive methods, including bottom tackle that snag objects; scientists believe that some poisons dissipate in water, but others can build up in food chain; fishing is now forbidden around four main dumping grounds, which hold estimated 300,000 tons of munitions; in other areas, vessels are required to keep special protective gear and medical kits on board; environmental and citizens' groups are demanding cleanup, but there is wide disagreement on how to approach problem; map; photo (M)

2003-06-20.title: Shimon Peres Is Elected Temporary Leader of Labor Party
2003-06-20.abstract: Shimon Peres is elected as temporary leader of Israel's Labor Party, which suffered its worst electoral defeat earlier in year; party leader Amram Mitzna resigned on May 4, and no clear successor has emerged (S)

2003-06-20.title: G.I. Dies as Military Ambulance Is Hit by Rocket-Propelled Grenade
2003-06-20.abstract: Soldier from 804th Medical Brigade is killed south of Baghdad when military ambulance in which he is traveling comes under fire from rocket-propelled grenade; soldier is 13th to die since small-scale, guerrilla-style attacks on American troops in Iraq began in May, several in recent days (M)

2003-06-21.title: Young Iraqis Face a Bewildering World. How They Fare Could Be Vital.
2003-06-21.abstract: Article profiles Akil Dair, 26-year-old part-time college student who lives with his family in Baghdad, Iraq; Dair and other young Iraqi men are being barraged by ideas, ideologies and political concepts they scarcely understand after life of isolation and war under Saddam Hussein; role models they adopt could be crucial to success of American effort to create democratic, friendly Iraq; photos (M)

2003-06-21.title: Moving in : Reading the fine print of lease can prevent misunderstandings
2003-06-21.abstract: PARIS—  Finding an acceptable apartment in a pleasant neighborhood abroad can be challenging enough for an expat who wants to hit the ground running in a foreign assignment. And while signing a lease may bring a sigh of relief, expats should take extra care to study local real estate customs and read the fine print of their lease if they want to prevent misunderstandings, unforeseen obligations or even the surprise of a sudden eviction.

2003-06-21.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: Disease Outbreak Confirmed
2003-06-21.abstract: Japanese encephalitis outbreak in Guangdong Province (China) is confirmed as regional authorities announce 18 deaths and 211 cases of disease; secrecy about outbreak, reminiscent of regional confusion regarding SARS outbreak, has caused Hong Kong officials to be concerned (S)

2003-06-21.title: CAPTURED OFFICIAL IS SAID TO TELL U.S. HUSSEIN SURVIVED
2003-06-21.abstract: Abid Hamid Mahmoud al-Tikriti, captured top aide to Saddam Hussein, reportedly tells US interrogators that Hussein and sons Uday and Qusay survived war and that he himself fled to Syria with sons after conflict; news reportedly sparks intense burst of clandestine US military activity aimed at capturing Hussein and his sons; American officials say conviction among loyalists that Hussein is still alive is motivating military resistance against US forces; photo (M)

2003-06-21.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Law Signed Ending Berlusconi Trial
2003-06-21.abstract: Italian President Carlo Azeglio Ciampi signs bill giving Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi immunity from prosecution while in office; legislation freezes his current bribery trial and avoids potential fallout from verdict before he becomes European Union president on July 1; photo (S)

2003-06-22.title: Overseer Says Iraq Economy Is Beginning To Recover
2003-06-22.abstract: American administrator L Paul Bremer 3d comes to World Economic Forum in Jordan with message of guarded optimism about Iraq's prospects; asserts that Iraq would soon resume oil exports and begin establishing economy based on private enterprise; seeks to counter image of an Iraq that is sliding into chaos, with rising attacks on American troops and frustration over inability to find Saddam Hussein or unconventional weapons; claims Iraq has already come long way in establishing law and order (M)

2003-06-22.title: Greek Officials Confiscate Explosives From Boat
2003-06-22.abstract: ATHENS, June 22 — Greek law enforcement officials said they found and confiscated 680 tons of explosives tonight from a boat off the country's western coast. The officials described the explosives as suspicious.

2003-06-22.title: Israelis Kill a Hamas Leader
2003-06-22.abstract: Israeli forces killed a leader of Hamas a day after Secretary of State Colin L. Powell tried to conclude a cease-fire agreement with Israeli and Palestinian leaders. Page 4.

2003-06-22.title: Killing of Hamas Chief Shouldn't Stop Peace Efforts, Powell Says
2003-06-22.abstract: Israel and the Palestinians continued to try to narrow their differences over the turnover of security throughout the Gaza Strip to the Palestinians.

2003-06-22.title: Anglicans Deeply Divided Over Appointment of Gay Bishop
2003-06-22.abstract: A large and vocal group of Anglicans are protesting their church's decision to name a gay priest, Dr. Jeffrey John, as the new Bishop of Reading.

2003-06-23.title: 'Drinking the same poison'
2003-06-23.abstract: Hamas leader Mahmud al-Sahar discusses his organization's strategy of violence, the conditions of a cease-fire, and cooperation with the Palestinian Autonomous Authorit

2003-06-23.title: New Gay Bishop Unsettles Church of England
2003-06-23.abstract: Large and vocal group of Anglicans protest their church's decision to name Dr Jeffrey John as new bishop of Reading, Eng; he is first openly homosexual bishop nominated by Church of England; action has stirred deeply divisive reaction that goes well beyond Reading; Dr John issues statement acknowledging that he has been in relationship for 27 years with male partner but avowing that they ceased having sex after church issued 1991 policy paper that permits members of congregation to pursue faithful homosexual relationships but demands celibacy from gay priests; photo (M)

2003-06-23.title: Israelis and Palestinians Seeking Accord on Gaza
2003-06-23.abstract: The talks are on whether and how Palestinian security forces will assume control from Israeli troops for policing the West Bank city of Bethlehem and parts of the Gaza Strip.

2003-06-23.title: U.S. Officials Doubt Hussein Was Killed in Raid Near Syria
2003-06-23.abstract: With no conclusive evidence found in the five days, the prospect that the former Iraqi leader had been killed was seen as a remote possibility at best.

2003-06-23.title: Jordan's King Shows Guests Some Flair
2003-06-23.abstract: King Abdullah II of Jordan, host of World Economic Forum conference, seems to have created his own style in four years since death of his father, King Hussein; in part he has extended his father's courtly manner, but he seems to want to blend dignity of his job with more open manner; some in kingdom fault him harshly for dissolving Parliament and postponing elections for two years; others credit him for holding elections at all in region ruled generally by fiat of one kind of another; photo (M)

2003-06-24.title: Temporary Truce by Hamas Expected, Palestinians Say
2003-06-24.abstract: Palestinian officials predict that militant group Hamas will soon agree to halt violence against Israel temporarily, acceding to request by Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas and to international pressure; Marwan Barghouti, rising leader of Yasir Arafat's Fatah movement who is imprisoned by Israel, is instrumental in seeking cease-fire; Israeli and Palestinian security officials report some progress toward determining how Palestinian security forces will assume control from Israeli troops for policing West Bank city of Bethlehem and parts of Gaza Strip; photos (M)

2003-06-24.title: WHO lifts Hong Kong from list of SARS areas
2003-06-24.abstract: HONG KONG:—  The World Health Organization on Monday removed Hong Kong from the list of SARS-infected areas, marking the end of a four-month crisis during which the deadly respiratory disease spread around the world.

2003-06-24.title: SYRIANS WOUNDED IN ATTACK BY U.S. ON CONVOY IN IRAQ
2003-06-24.abstract: US Special Operations forces engage in firefight with several Syrian guards during American attack on convoy suspected of carrying fugitive Iraqi officials near Iraqi border with Syria on June 18; at least one of Iraqi vehicles destroyed in attack is hit by American attack helicopters on Syrian side of border; five Syrian guards are wounded, and three of them remain in American custody for medical treatment; American officials detain as many as 20 Iraqis, but do not provide any details about their identities; US has not yet discussed matter with Syrian government because so much remains unclear, including whether Syrian guards were trying to help or hinder American effort to halt convoy; photos; map (M)

2003-06-24.title: Bush Declares Student an Enemy Combatant
2003-06-24.abstract: Pres Bush removes Ali Saleh Kahlah al-Marri, Qatari student, from criminal justice system and declares him enemy combatant after prosecutors say new evidence links him to another round of terrorist plots by Al Qaeda after Sept 11; Marri is moved from prison in Illinois to military brig in South Carolina, where he can be held indefinitely without access to lawyer unless military decides to bring charges; critics of detention policies say move adds to confusion over using array of military, criminal and civil measures against people considered terrorism suspects; photo (M)

2003-06-24.title: A Rash of Pipeline Fires Is Keeping Workers Busy
2003-06-24.abstract: Series of pipeline explosions, the work of expert saboteurs, hampers efforts to restore oil production in Iraq, which is now only third of prewar levels; looting of equipment slows repair work; photo (M)

2003-06-25.title: Hussein's Top Aide Got Belarussian Passports, U.S. Officials Say
2003-06-25.abstract: US officials say Abid Hamid Mahmoud al-Tikriti, senior lieutanant to Saddam Hussein, obtained Belarussian passports for himself and others, possibly including Hussein's sons, in Syria, where Mahmoud fled after war; say Mahmoud never claimed Hussein fled to Syria, only that he saw him alive after American attack on April 7 that was attempt to kill him; believe Hussein is still in Iraq; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld and Gen Richard B Myers defend June 18 raid on Iraqi convoy along Syrian border, which was prompted by Mahmoud's account of Iraqi officials' escape into Syria (M)

2003-06-25.title: Latest Peace Hopes Thwarted on Africa's Battlefields
2003-06-25.abstract: Latest hopes for peace in Bunia, northeastern provincial town in Congo, and Liberia are dealt setbacks; multinational force that was to declare Bunia a gun-free zone wants more time for talks with militia that now controls town; chief rebel group in Liberia says it will boycott peace talks until Pres Charles Taylor steps down, and there are reports of renewed fighting near Monrovia, prompting yet another exodus of terrified civilians from capital; map (M)

2003-06-25.title: U.S. Silent on Israeli Move
2003-06-25.abstract: Bush administration says it will not comment on arrest by Israel of 130 Palestinians in Hebron until American experts can inquire about who was detained and what evidence lead Israel to make arrests (S)

2003-06-25.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Military Breaks Silence
2003-06-25.abstract: Mexican military, fulfilling new freedom of information law, discloses to El Universal Mexico City newspaper that 299 soldiers have been court-martialed since December 2000; 210 of those tried were convicted on variety of charges (S)

2003-06-25.title: Deaths of Military Policemen Raise New Doubts Back Home in Britain
2003-06-25.abstract: Killing of six British military policemen in southern Iraq and ambush of British paratroopers threaten to raise new questions among Britons about deployment of their troops alongside US forces; adds to political woes confronting Prime Min Tony Blair over his reasons for sending British troops to Iraq in first place; several antiwar members of Parliament urge deployment of United Nations peacekeepers to replace British and American troops; Foreign Sec Jack Straw says dossier of supposed evidence that Iraq possessed illicit weapons, which Blair used to justify Iraqi invasion, has become embarrassment and should never have been published (M)

2003-06-26.title: Worth a (bike) trip, 100 years later
2003-06-26.abstract: MONTGERON, France:—  The steak at the Reveil Matin in the Paris suburb of Montgeron is tender, the house wine thin but acceptable, the service no worse than the usual inattentive, the bill a bit less than it would be in a more chic and higher-rent restaurant in the capital.

2003-06-26.title: World Briefing | Africa: Kenya: Embassy Reopens
2003-06-26.abstract: American Embassy in Nairobi (Kenya) reopens after closure for several days amid concerns of terrorist attack; Kenyan and Tanzanian officials find fault with American and British travel warnings to East Africa made during state of alert (S)

2003-06-26.title: World Briefing | Europe: Poland: Abortion Boat Fined
2003-06-26.abstract: Mobile abortion clinic housed on Dutch ship and organized by Women on Waves, women's rights group, is fined $3,000 by Wladyslawowo (Poland) port authorities for failing to follow docking instructions that required ship to tie up away from protesters; boat brings birth control and pregnancy counseling to countries where abortion is illegal; boards women wanting abortions and takes them to international waters to perform procedure (S)

2003-06-26.title: Al Qaeda Remains a Global Threat, U.N. Report Says
2003-06-26.abstract: A new generation of Al Qaeda-trained terrorists as well as veterans of the Islamic extremist group were said to continue to threaten the global community.

2003-06-26.title: Blair Says Reinforcements May Go to Iraq
2003-06-26.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair, responding to killing of six British soldiers in disputed circumstances in southern Iraq, pledges to maintain British military presence there and to send reinforcements if needed; pledge seems designed to head off suggestions by some legislators that Britain should begin contemplating 'exit strategy' (M)

2003-06-27.title: Bush Calls for Changes in Africa To End Wars and Promote Trade
2003-06-27.abstract: Pres Bush outlines ambitious agenda for advancing peace and prosperity in Africa; pledges $100 million to help countries fight terrorism and makes case for expanded trade as most powerful engine for fighting poverty on continent; speaks to US-Africa Business Summit, group of African leaders, business executives and investors, 11 days before his scheduled first trip as president to sub-Saharan Africa; his speech is striking for its blunt calls for change in nations that have been wracked by violence; he demands that Pres Charles Taylor of Liberia step down to avert further bloodshed in his country; he calls for change of government and 'return to democracy' in Zimbabwe, which is ruled by Robert Mugabe; pledges active role in bringing peace to Sudan, saying he has asked his special envoy to Sudan John C Danforth to travel there to bring end to conflict (M)

2003-06-27.title: Saudi Arabia Arrests a Suspect in the Riyadh Bombings
2003-06-27.abstract: Saudi Arabia arrests senior figure in Al Qaeda who is believed to have played crucial role in planning and carrying out May bombings in Riyadh that killed 34 people, including 8 Americans; senior American and Saudi government officials say they believe same man, Ali Abd al-Rahman al-Faqasi Al-Ghamdi, had been planning further operations against American targets; Ghamdi's name and picture were included among those of 19 men sought in connection with arms caches seized in Riyadh several days before attack; photo (M)

2003-06-27.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ireland: Rules For Clergy And Children
2003-06-27.abstract: Ferns Diocese in County Wexford, center of Ireland's clerical sex abuse scandal, directs priests to never be alone with single child or adolescent and to use public rooms with windows when meeting with groups of children or teenagers (S)

2003-06-27.title: Support for Britain's Labor Party Eroding, Poll Says
2003-06-27.abstract: As a poll showed public disenchantment with the Labor Party, Foreign Secretary Jack Straw said today that he stood by the accuracy of British government intelligence on Iraqi weapons.

2003-06-27.title: Arab Bomber, Freed After 27 Years, Longs for Peace but Has No Regrets
2003-06-27.abstract: Ahmed Jubarah, who helped plant bomb that killed 13 people and wounded more than 60 in Jerusalem in 1975, is released from jail after 27 years in concession by Israel to encourage new peace process; he was longest-serving Palestinian prisoner; he has emerged as symbol of popular Palestinian movement for release of prisoners; he has not expressed any shame or remorse for randomly killing civilians, but he says he longs for peace; says only after Israel agrees to Palestinian state free of settlements can violence end; photo (M)

2003-06-28.title: Palestinians Urge Smaller Factions to Join Truce
2003-06-28.abstract: Palestinian leaders reached beyond Hamas and Islamic Jihad today to persuade other, smaller factions to agree to a complete suspension of violence against Israelis.

2003-06-28.title: World Briefing | Americas: Argentina: Chief Justice Leaves
2003-06-28.abstract: Julio Nazareno, Argentine Supreme Court chief justice, resigns less than two weeks before start of his impeachment trial for 22 counts of malfeasance and illegal conduct; President Nestor Kirchner had accused him of attempted political blackmail (S)

2003-06-28.title: Pakistanis Protest Offer Of Iraq Force
2003-06-28.abstract: Protests arise in Pakistan after Pres Pervez Musharraf, after meeting with Pres Bush at Camp David, suggests that Pakistani troops might be sent to Iraq if United States would finance the expedition; several politicians say move would make Pakistan Army nothing more than mercenary force (M)

2003-06-28.title: Powell Hails Man Who Cited Pressure on Iraq Data
2003-06-28.abstract: Secretary of State Colin Powell offers public support for Christian Westermann, department chemical and biological weapons expert who told Congress that he felt pressured to make intelligence reports on Iraq and other matters conform to Bush administration policy (M)

2003-06-28.title: Rain Forest Is Losing Ground Faster in Amazon, Photos Show
2003-06-28.abstract: New satellite images show that Amazon rain forest is disappearing at increasing rate--with about 10,000 square miles lost mainly to pasture land, plantations and illegal logging in 12-month period ended last August; scientists and environmentalists say about 20 percent of Amazon has already vanished and that rain forest could be wiped out in 80 years; Brazilian Environment Min Marina Silva promises emergency action but does not offer specific proposals (M)

2003-06-29.title: Bodies of G.I.'s, Missing 3 Days, Found in Iraq
2003-06-29.abstract: Bodies of two American soldiers missing in Iraq for 3 days are found, miles apart from their Humvee; military refuses to give any details about how men died or to answer speculation that they might have been abducted; two soldiers are Sgt First Class Gladimir Philippe and Pfc Kevin Ott; they disappeared near town of Balad, 20 miles from Baghdad, in area near strongholds of conservative Sunni Muslims; Army criminal investigators have been assigned to determine what happed at scene, to find out how two heavily armed soldiers and their armored military vehicle could have been taken without report of struggle or of gunfire; photos; map (M)

2003-06-29.title: An Aging Europe May Find Itself on the Sidelines
2003-06-29.abstract: Population that is both living longer and producing fewer children is beginning to change some of basics of both social and political life in Germany and elsewhere in Europe; many experts see government bids to cut pensions and other social services as harbinger of demographic shift with important implications for European societies as whole; median age is expected to rise from 37.7 to 52.3 in 2050 in Europe, compared with only 35.4 in United States, meaning European economic and military power will decline in world that is largely becoming younger; European population is shrinking as well; Europe seems to face choice of allowing more immigration or accepting less influential role in world affairs; graph of median age and fertility rate; photos (M)

2003-06-29.title: American Forces Carry Out Raids in Central Iraq
2003-06-29.abstract: The raids, which involved thousands of soldiers and hundreds of tanks and armored vehicles, resulted in more than 60 arrests.

2003-06-29.title: A Boom From Oil Transforms a Russian Island
British Foreign Secretary Urges Iran to Accept Addition to Arms Treaty
2003-06-29.abstract: Britain's foreign secretary urged Iran to sign an additional protocol that would open the way to more aggressive U.N. inspections of its nuclear sites.

2003-06-30.title: Liberia War Chokes the Life of Monrovia
2003-06-30.abstract: Liberian capital, Monrovia, remains tense and uncertain as residents wait to see how long latest cease fire will hold; city bears scars of more than decade-long string of battles, its architecture and infrastructure ruined and its most basic functions in collapse; calls for international intervention have grown louder in recent days, as have questions about how to get Pres Charles Taylor to finally relinquish power; map (M)

2003-06-30.title: Apathetic Voters Seem Likely To Prolong Mexican Stalemate
2003-06-30.abstract: Midterm elections will be first national vote since Mexico's transition to multiparty rule, with control of Congress and future of Pres Vicente Fox hanging in balance; warring parties are spending heavily in quest for votes, yet polls suggest that half of electorate will sit election out; those who will vote seem set to keep power deeply divided, giving neither Fox nor any party upper hand and all but ensuring three more years of political stagnation until 2006 presidential election; photo (M)

2003-06-30.title: German Cause Célèbre: A Teacher's Head Scarf
2003-06-30.abstract: Lawsuit brought by Fereshta Ludin over being denied public teaching job because she wears Islamic head scarf comes before Germany's highest court; dispute has divided public opinion and become touchstone for German anxieties about growing Islamic minority (M)

2003-06-30.title: Arab World Greets Truce With Guarded Optimism
2003-06-30.abstract: The Arab world greeted the news with a wary optimism today, tempered by long frustration over the conflict.

2003-06-30.title: U.S. Chill Flattens Mood at French Wine Fair
2003-06-30.abstract: American wine merchants are mostly absent from France's largest wine fair in Bordeaux, confirming to many that American ill will toward France continues and signaling further decline in already depressed French wine exports to US; chart; photo (M)
